title: Federal Government Invests in Zero Emission Transit Planning Projects
content: The federal government has invested nearly $850,000 through the Zero Emission Transit Fund (ZETF) to help eight Ontario communities transition to electric buses, with local contributions totaling $210,494. The funding supports planning studies on fleet electrification, including route mapping, charging infrastructure, timelines, and climate resilience assessments. This initiative aligns with Canada's net-zero emissions goals and complements broader transit investments under the Canada Public Transit Fund, which provides $3 billion annually to support clean, affordable, and sustainable transportation.
author: Analysts Account
